label: Magnetic resonance imaging
comments: Technique using magnetic fields and radio waves to generate images of organs.

label: X-ray microtomography
exactSynonyms: Computed microtomography, High-resolution micro-CT, Micro computed tomography, Micro-computed tomography, micro CT, micro-CT, µCT

label: Array tomography
comments: Can be just EM or anything. But may also combine optical and electron microscopy of ultra thin serial sections.

label: Optical projection tomography
exactSynonyms: OPT, OPT, Optical tomography, Optical tomography
comments: OPT is a form of tomography using visible light to generate 3D image volumes based on absorption (label-free) or fluorescence.

label: Optical projection tomography
exactSynonyms: OPT, OPT, Optical tomography, Optical tomography
comments: OPT is a form of tomography using visible light to generate 3D image volumes based on absorption (label-free) or fluorescence.

label: High-content analysis
seeAlsos: In simple terms, high-content screening (HCS) is the combination of high-throughput screening (HTS) applied to high-content analysis (HCA)., In simple terms, high-content screening (HCS) is the combination of high-throughput screening (HTS) applied to high-content analysis (HCA).

label: Correlative light and electron microscopy
comments: Correlative light and electron microscopy is the combination of light microscopy (typically fluorescence microscopy) and electron microscopy of the same sample.

label: Super-resolution microscopy
comments: Group of microscopy techniques that circumvent the diffration limit and therefore allow for a greater spatial resolution  limit.
seeAlsos: There are rare exceptions of super-resolution microscopy, such as scanning near-field optical microscopy (SNOM), that do not necessarily rely on fluorescence., There are rare exceptions of super-resolution microscopy, such as scanning near-field optical microscopy (SNOM), that do not necessarily rely on fluorescence.

label: Single molecule localisation microscopy
comments: SMLM is based on a temporal separation of the emission of fluorescent molecules, allowing a fitting and precise localisation of these molecules.

label: Structured illumination microscopy
comments: A patterned illumination interferes with the sample and creates moire fringes which are further processed by an algorithm to increase spatial resolution.
seeAlsos: Structured illumination microscopy includes also techniques that do not rely on fluorescence.

label: Stimulated emission depletion microscopy
comments: An increase in spatial resolution is obtained thanks to stimulated emission properties of fluorophores illuminated simultaneously by two lasers.

label: Fluorescence correlation spectroscopy
comments: FCS measures fluctuations of fluorescence in a volume, providing after analysis physical parameters of the sample such as diffusion coefficient or concentration.

label: Photoablation
comments: This technique allows the selective cutting of parts of samples without thermal damage.

label: Point-scanning microscopy
comments: Microscopy techniques relying on the point by point imaging of the entire sample. This is typically performed with point illumination of the sample.

label: Transmission light microscopy
comments: Techniques in microscopy in which the sample is placed between the illumination source and the observer or recording device.

label: Stereoscopy
comments: Stereoscopy is a non-digital technique creating the illusion of depth in an image.

label: Adaptive microscopy
seeAlsos: Intelligent microscopy uses artificial intelligence; smart microscopy is using a defined algorithm to adapt microscope configuration or imaging parameters. (Robert Haase & consensus)

label: Fixed sample imaging
comments: Imaging of biological tissues preserved by chemicals or temperature.

label: Spectroscopy
comments:
Spectroscopy refers to the study of how radiated energy and matter interact. The energy is absorbed by the matter, creating an excited state. The interaction creates some form of electromagnetic waves (EM), often in the form of visible light, such as sparks.

Spectrometry deals with the measurement of a specific spectrum; essentially the application of spectroscopy so that there are quantifiable results that can then be assessed.

label: Bioluminescence imaging
comments: Recording of native light emission by organism expressing a light emitting molecule.

label: Live sample imaging
comments: imaging of samples in conditions that keeps them alive, allowing the study of dynamic changes.

label: In-vitro imaging
comments: Imaging of tissues fixed or kept in physiological but artificial conditions.

label: In-vivo imaging
comments: imaging in the living body of a plant or an animal

label: Intravital imaging
comments: Imaging of live processes performed within living animals, typically by multi-photon imaging techniques.

label: Multimodal imaging
seeAlsos: Utilizing different imaging modalities in combination, Utilizing different imaging modalities in combination
